DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, DEC-07-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANTS BARREL HOUSE TAVERN, CHRIS HENRY, APPLE ANNIE'S, LLC, AND HERRINGBONE TAVERN, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY PLAINTIFFS DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DISCOVERY IS STAYED WITHOUT PREJUDICE PENDING THE COURT'S DETERMINATON AS TO THE ISSUE OF ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTIONA AS THERE IS A CONTRACTUAL PROVISION IN THIS CASE REQUIRING ARBITRATION OR MEDIATION. THE COURT MAKES NO DECISION ON THE MERITS AS TO WHETHER THE SUBPOENA IS VALID OR NOT. THE COURT INSTRUCTS DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO OPPOSING COUNSEL AND TO THE COURT AT RKANE1089@AOL.COM. THE PROPOSED ORDER MAY INCLUDE THE INTENDED HEARING DATE ON DEFENDANTS' MOTION REGARDING ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION, AND MAY INCLUDE LANGUAGE THAT THE COPY SERVICE IS TO HOLD ON TO THE SUBPOENAED DOCUMENTS UNTIL A FURTHER ORDER OF THE COURT. IF THE PROPOSED ORDER IS NOT APPROVED AS TO FORM, BOTH SIDES MAY SUBMIT THEIR OWN SEPARATE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROBERT KANE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration And To Stay Civil Proceedings [Ccp 1290]
Off Calendar Jan-20-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

Defendants' motion to compel mediation is denied. Mediation is voluntary and there is no authority that allows the Court to compel that relief. (See Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 536, 540 [court erred in compelling mediation].) For complete details of ruling and findings, see Word (.doc) minutes and/or written order.

Written order is signed.

Off Calendar Dec-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALESHIA M. WHITE, Esq. of Berliner Cohen, LLP (Tel: 408-286-5800) for Plaintiffs Tarantino's Inc., Timothy J. McDonnel, and Gary Burns;
Atty. GEORGE G. BENETATOS, Esq. of Law Office of George G. Benetatos (Tel: 415-203.2296) for Defendants Chris Henry, Herringbone Tavern, Inc., dba Tarantino's, Barrel House Tavern, and Apple Annie's, LLC dba Tommy's Joynt; and for Cross-Complainant Herringbone Tavern, Inc., dba Tarantino's.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS BARREL HOUSE TAVERN, CHRIS HENRY, APPLE ANNIE'S, LLC, AND HERRINGBONE TAVERN, INC.'s Motion To Quash Or Modify Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides all authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Discovery is stayed without prejudice pending the Court's determinaton as to the issue of alternative dispute resolutiona as there is a contractual provision in this case requiring arbitration or mediation. The Court makes no decision on the merits as to whether the subpoena is valid or not.

The Court instructs defense counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to opposing counsel and to the Court at rkane1089@aol.com. The proposed order may include the intended hearing date on defendants' motion regarding alternative dispute resolution, and may include language that the copy service is to hold on to the subpoenaed documents until a further order of the Court. If the proposed order is not approved as to form, both sides may submit their own separate proposed order.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
